title: Threads, Atomics, Data Races, and Safe Reclamation · Independent C Trial
summary: An independent maintainer ports the threaded lifetime and reclamation proof to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of threaded lifetime and reclamation proof with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.
Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.
Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Data Race Undefined
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 1810. Produce bounded C evidence to model publication and reclamation state without claiming thread proof; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Threadsanitizer Limit
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Threadsanitizer Limit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 8571. Produce bounded C evidence to model publication and reclamation state without claiming thread proof; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Threadsanitizer Limit.
steps.why: Retrieving Threadsanitizer Limit through the concrete threaded lifetime and reclamation proof case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Threadsanitizer Limit, keep Threadsanitizer Limit connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Threadsanitizer Limit, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Threadsanitizer Limit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Threadsanitizer Limit, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A lab native-systems team handles memory case 8571. Produce bounded C evidence to model publication and reclamation state without claiming thread proof; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Threadsanitizer Limit, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A clean ThreadSanitizer run proves all schedules race-free.
